mnemonics.title: Basic Operations Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Remember 6 basic operations using: S.P.U.D.C.R (Select, Project, Union, Difference, Cartesian, Rename) - Think 'SPUD CR' as a food item

mnemonics.title: Selection vs Projection
mnemonics.content: Selection (σ) = S elects ROWS (like a filter); Projection (π) = P rojects COLUMNS (like a spotlight)

mnemonics.title: Join Types
mnemonics.content: NATURAL = Same names; THETA = Any condition; EQUI = Equal only; OUTER = Keep orphans

acronyms.usage: Theoretical query language for relational databases
Extracts rows based on condition: σcondition(relation)
Combines related tuples from two relations
Finds tuples matching ALL tuples in another relation

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a filter funnel - pouring data through, only rows matching condition pass through
Think of a projector light shining on specific columns, highlighting only those while hiding others
Imagine a grid/matrix where every row from table A meets every row from table B
Two puzzle pieces connecting through matching keys/columns
Think of it as 'AND' logic - tuple must match ALL conditions like checking all boxes in a checklist

analogies.analogy: Like using WHERE clause in SQL or filtering emails by sender - keeps only matching rows
Like choosing specific columns in Excel or selecting only name and phone from contacts
Like creating all possible combinations in a schedule - every employee with every shift
Like matching students to courses based on student ID - connecting through common identifier
Like finding students who passed ALL subjects - must pass each one individually
Like a class photo where everyone is included even if they didn't participate in group activity - those left out stand aside but are still in photo
